title: Filter to show items that other items are dependent on
excerpt: I would like to be able to filter to see items that other items are dependent on.  So if item B is dependent on item A, I would like to filter to see A (not B) because A is holding up B.  I don’t think this is currently …

posts.author: Usernamegame (Jake N)
posts.cookedHtml: I would like to be able to filter to see items that other items are dependent on. So if item B is dependent on item A, I would like to filter to see A (not B) because A is holding up B. I don’t think this is currently possible but it seems to be handy for project management.

posts.author: mondayguru (Cole Casper)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello, I had the opposite problem. I wanted to see all successors of one item. I was able to use following filter:

Where [Dependent On] [Is] [Item A]

This allowed me to see all items dependent on one item I was looking to change. I am now having the issue that I have one successor (Item B) with multiple predecessors (Items A1, A2, A3, etc.). I want to visualise in my gantt chart only Item B and its predecessors. If anyone has any suggestions, let me know !

Additionally, it would be nice if monday.com had the ability to show on Item A a column of all successors (Items B1, B2, B3, etc.).
